items.title: PM’s Dept: Luxury vehicle seen used by Anwar an existing govt asset, official car under maintenance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — A luxury vehicle used by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim recently is an existing government asset and not a new official vehicle. “For that purpose, this vehicle must meet specially designed security specifications to withstand high-calibre ballistic threats, explosions, and modern security risks including drone threats. “The use of the vehicle by the Prime Minister is temporary on the advice and security assessment of the Royal Malaysian Police (PDRM),” the statement said.

items.title: Did you see this hit-and-run? Police seek witnesses after man dies changing punctured tyre on Cheras-Kajang Highway
items.summary: The driver of the vehicle then fled the scene without stopping, police said today. Kajang district police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said preliminary investigations found that the victim had pulled his silver Toyota Innova onto the left shoulder after one of its tyres burst. “While the victim was removing the bucket, another red Toyota Innova approached from behind, struck him and continued driving without stopping,” Naazron said in a statement.

items.title: Anwar urges Johor early voters to choose Pakatan as polling begins
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 7 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today extended his best wishes to all early voters casting their ballots in the 16th Johor state election. “Happy voting to all early voters in the Johor state election. A total of 24,751 eligible early voters will cast their ballots at 64 polling centres across the state today.

items.title: #NSTviral: BMW alleged to have evaded roadblock, crashes near Gua Musang highway
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: An older-model white BMW attempting to evade a roadblock lost control and slammed into a concrete wall near a petrol station in Kuala Lipis. The incident, which occurred near a Shell station just before the Gua Musang highway, was captured on camera and uploaded to Facebook by user Naz Kdas. "An old BMW fled a roadblock, speeding like crazy.

items.title: Early voting for Johor election begins at 64 centres statewide
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 7 — A total of 64 early voting centres for the 16th Johor state election opened at 8am today to enable 24,751 members of the security forces and their spouses to fulfil their responsibilities. According to Election Commission (EC) statistics, a total of 12,041 members of the Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) and their spouses will carry out their responsibilities at 11 designated polling centres. The remaining 12,710 voters, comprising Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) personnel and General Operations Force (GOF) members and their spouses, are scheduled to cast their votes at 53 polling centres.

items.title: Ringgit opens slightly higher against greenback amid focus on Fed outlook
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened slightly higher against the US dollar on Tuesday amid continued focus on the United States (US) Federal Reserve's (Fed) interest rate outlook. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said investors remained focused on the Fed, while the resilience of the US services sector was expected to keep the US dollar well supported. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Police seek witnesses after fatal hit-and-run on Cheras-Kajang Highway
items.summary: KAJANG: Police are seeking witnesses to a fatal hit-and-run accident involving a pedestrian and a car at Km13 of the Cheras–Kajang Expressway last night. District police chief said the incident occurred about 10pm yesterday when a 46-year-old man was struck by a red Toyota Innova. As he was removing the bucket, the red Toyota Innova struck him and sped off without stopping.

items.title: Johor polls: 24,751 early voters set to cast ballots today
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 7 — A total of 24,751 early voters eligible to vote in the 16th Johor state election will cast their ballots today at 64 gazetted polling centres across the state. All 53 police polling centres and 11 military polling centres were set to open simultaneously at 8am. Polling centres with small electorates, including Tenang Police Station in the Tenang state constituency, with only six voters, and Bukit Besar Police Station in the Pasir Raja constituency, with 11 voters, are expected to close as early as noon.

items.title: Defence minister: Malaysia to receive first LCS on time despite missing anti-ship missiles
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — Malaysia will take delivery of its first Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) as scheduled in December despite the vessel being delivered without its planned anti-ship missile system following the cancellation of its Naval Strike Missile (NSM) procurement, Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said. The only capability it currently lacks is the surface-to-surface missile,” he said. Khaled said the vessel would still be equipped with a 57mm naval gun, anti-submarine and anti-air warfare systems, as well as electronic warfare capabilities despite the absence of its surface-to-surface missile system.

items.title: Samsung expects 1,800pct leap in quarterly operating profit on AI boom
items.summary: SEOUL: South Korean technology giant Samsung Electronics forecast on Tuesday a roughly 19-fold jump in second-quarter operating profit from a year earlier, buoyed by sustained AI-driven demand for memory chips. Frenzied global demand for advanced memory chips used in data centres for artificial intelligence has already helped South Korean semiconductor giants post record profits this year. Revenue likely rose 129 per cent to 171 trillion won, Samsung said.

items.title: Big US banks explore Fiserv network deal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: US banks, including JPMorgan and Bank of America, have in recent months held early talks about a deal to buy a network owned by the payments firm Fiserv that could allow them to bypass federal debit-card fee caps, the Wall Street Journal reported on Monday, citing sources. However, banks would be exempt from that cap if they also own the network, the WSJ report said. Other banks that have held preliminary and tentative discussions include Wells Fargo and PNC Financial Services Group, the report added.

items.title: Government mulls national registry for domestic violence offenders
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is studying the establishment of a national Domestic Violence Offender Registry to track repeat offenders and strengthen protection for women and children. "The proposed registry is aimed at strengthening prevention, enforcement and protection efforts for victims of domestic violence. Nancy added that the Domestic Violence Committee would continue to coordinate, monitor and discuss policies, programmes and initiatives among relevant agencies to improve the national response to domestic violence.

items.title: Court orders MDEC to pay ex-manager over RM500k for constructive dismissal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Digital Economy Corporation (MDEC) has been ordered to pay RM514,836 to a former senior manager after the Industrial Court ruled that he was constructively dismissed. Industrial Court chairman Andersen Ong held that MDEC had breached the implied term of mutual trust and confidence through a series of actions that undermined Adlin Shah Abdul Latiff's role, authority, professional standing and dignity, leaving him with no reasonable option but to resign. Adlin joined MDEC on a fixed-term contract in August 2008 before being confirmed as a permanent employee in 2010.

items.title: Lim Kit Siang urges Johoreans not to split Pakatan vote in ‘test of the Malaysian Dream’
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 7 — DAP veteran Tan Sri Lim Kit Siang has called on Johoreans not to split Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) vote in the upcoming Johor state election, saying it is crucial to realising the Malaysian Dream. “This will also put an end to the Malaysian Dream,” he said during his brief appearance at a PH campaign event in Taman Perling here last night. Lim, who has retired from active politics, described the upcoming Johor state election as a test of the Malaysian Dream.

items.title: Stocks surge on chip news, oil holds at pre-war levels
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Wall Street surged on Monday and oil prices settled around pre-Iran war levels, as investors eyed new developments in the AI and chipmaker sector ahead of corporate earnings. All three major U.S. indexes ended the day higher. OIL STEADY Oil prices were flat on Monday, as prices lingered around pre-Iran war levels.

items.title: Ringgit seen regaining ground despite recent pullback [WATCH]
items.summary: They believe external factors, particularly the direction of United States (US) monetary policy and broader global risk sentiment, will remain the dominant drivers of the ringgit. This reinforces economists' view that Malaysia's underlying fundamentals remain intact despite short-term external headwinds. Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said investors remain cautious as they await greater clarity on the US Federal Reserve's interest rate direction.

items.title: Cuba begins to restore power to Havana following nationwide blackout
items.summary: HAVANA: Cuba began to slowly restore power on Monday after the country's national electric grid collapsed earlier in the day, the latest blow to an island already suffering from severe energy, fuel and medicine shortages. Officials have not yet said what caused the grid to collapse. Cuba has for months suffered from hours-long and, more recently, days-long power outages linked in part to a decrepit grid and a US-imposed oil blockade that has cut off the island's fuel supply.

items.title: 24,751 early voters head to Johor polls [WATCH]
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A total of 24,751 early voters eligible to vote in the 16th Johor election will cast their ballots today at 64 gazetted polling centres across the state. All 53 police polling centres and 11 military polling centres will open simultaneously at 8 am. Polling centres with small electorates, including Tenang Police Station in the Tenang state constituency, with only six voters, and Bukit Besar Police Station in the Pasir Raja constituency, with 11 voters, are expected to close as early as noon.

items.title: Malaysia's FMM, Hong Kong productivity council deepen smart manufacturing push
items.summary: HONG KONG: The Federation of Malaysian Manufacturing (FMM) plans to scale up its collaboration with Hong Kong Productivity Council (HKPC) to accelerate the adoption of smart manufacturing and Industry 4.0 among enterprises in both economies. FMM president Jacob Lee Chor Kok said both parties have been aggressive in their efforts to identify factories in Malaysia that Hong Kong enterprises can assist to improve productivity among manufacturers in the country. A greater integration of supply chain between both economies enables Malaysia to seek alternative supply sources amid limited options during the escalating geopolitical tensions.

items.title: Race to recover bodies ahead of Venezuela quake cleanup
items.summary: CARABALLEDA: The international rescue teams are packing up and heavy excavators are clearing rubble left by Venezuela's earthquakes. Watching volunteers pick through the crushed remains of his 12-storey apartment building, Alvarado knows his mother, father and older brother are inside. Their third-floor apartment now sits at eye level, crushed under piles of concertinaed concrete slabs from the OPP 26 building in coastal Caraballeda, one of the districts hardest hit by the quakes.

items.title: MB: Mid-term review to find and fix issues
items.summary: IPOH: The state government will undertake a mid-term review of the Perak Sejahtera 2030 blueprint to ensure the state's long-term development agenda remains relevant and aligned with current economic realities. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said the review would be carried out by Institut Darul Ridzuan (IDR) and the State Economic Planning Unit (Upen) following the appointment of Dr Zulqarnain Mohamad as IDR's chief executive officer. He said the review would also assess whether the economic focus assigned to each district remained appropriate.

items.title: Australia's Lynas signs deal with South Korea's JS Link to develop Malaysian magnet factory
items.summary: Lynas Rare Earths said on Tuesday it has signed a partnership deal with South Korea's JS Link to develop a magnet factory in Malaysia. The companies have also agreed that the Australian rare-earths producer will supply rare-earth materials to JS Link's magnet factory in South Korea and the planned factory in Malaysia until January 2038. The partnership follows a magnet manufacturing deal between the two companies last year.

items.title: Delivering results, building confidence
items.summary: THE Perak Sejahtera 2030 blueprint remains firmly on track, with its implementation beginning to translate the state's economic growth into tangible benefits for the people. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said the blueprint has over the past four years become the state's key development framework for strengthening the economy, attracting investments, creating jobs and improving the people's well-being. Saarani said the progress was reflected in the state's economic performance, with Perak recording gross domestic product (GDP) growth of 5.7 per cent last year, surpassing the national growth rate of 5.2 per cent.

items.title: From Singapore commutes to affordable housing: What Johor election manifestos promise voters
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — As the race for the 16th Johor State Election enters its critical phase, the primary political contenders have released competing manifestos that detail different economic and social blueprints. From economic policies and welfare programmes to governance reforms and development plans, each manifesto presents a distinct strategy to address Johor’s economic and social challenges. Barisan Nasional (BN) is anchoring its campaign on the manifesto “ Maju Johor , Stability Maintained, Progress Continued”.

items.title: Hate speech outruns facts on social media because ‘we react strongly to emotional content’, say experts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 7 — With the Online Safety Act 2025 now in force in Malaysia, discussions are continuing on how best to curb hate speech, divisive rhetoric and misinformation on social media platforms. Election periods are usually the time when there is an uptick in politically-driven negative content and fake news on social media. The problem, it seems, is the amplification effect of social media platforms.

items.title: 'Year 4 Learning Matrix is for early intervention'
items.summary: THE 2026 Year Four Learning Matrix, scheduled for Oct 6-8, will be used to identify learning needs and guide early intervention, not rank pupils or schools, said the Education Ministry. Education Ministry Examinations Board director Ab Aziz Mamat said the learning matrix would be conducted as "an assessment for learning", enabling teachers, schools and parents to identify pupils' mastery levels and learning gaps before they enter secondary school. Ab Aziz said the findings would also help the ministry identify learning gaps nationwide and evaluate the education system's effectiveness to support evidence-based policymaking.

items.title: Russian strikes kill 28 in Kyiv area, exposing Ukraine air-defence shortages
items.summary: KYIV: Russia hammered Kyiv and the surrounding region with missiles and drones early on Monday, killing at least 28 people and exposing Ukraine's critical shortage of US-made air-defence interceptors, officials said. Ukraine's military was unable to down any of the 23 ballistic missiles fired by Russia, according to air force data, reflecting its increasing vulnerability to Moscow's strikes as stocks of its prized Patriot missiles run out. Ukrainian air force data shows air defences shot down just four of 49 ballistic missiles in July.

items.title: Pulau Tuba gets sea ambulance, emergency birthing unit
items.summary: THE Health Ministry has rolled out a RM1.45 million sea ambulance and emergency birthing unit (EBU) on Pulau Tuba for the benefit of more than 5,000 island residents. The ministry said in a statement yesterday the 12-metre sea ambulance, which began operations on May 20, was custom-built for patient transport and equipped with modern emergency care facilities. The sea ambulance now enables faster, safer and more efficient patient transfers, strengthening emergency response for the entire island community, it added.

items.title: U Mobile sets up advisory panel for enterprise innovation platform
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: U Mobile Sdn Bhd has roped in industry leaders in artificial intelligence (AI), enterprise innovation, small and medium enterprise (SME) development and digital ecosystems to strengthen its enterprise innovation platform (EIP). Together, they formed an advisory panel to guide the EIP, which is U Mobile's open innovation platform designed to connect enterprises, technology partners, startups, academia and ecosystem stakeholders to accelerate innovation and digital transformation. The advisory panel will offer insights on key innovation priorities, evolving enterprise demands and opportunities to strengthen ecosystem partnerships.

items.title: Charlie Kirk widow comes face-to-face with alleged killer
items.summary: LOS ANGELES: The widow of assassinated right-wing activist Charlie Kirk came face-to-face Monday with the man alleged to have killed him, during a court hearing in the western United States. Erika Kirk was among those attending a preliminary hearing that will determine if there is enough evidence for 23-year-old Tyler Robinson to face trial over the September 2025 shooting on a Utah university campus. Erika Kirk, who was accompanied in the Utah court by Donald Trump Jr and other high-profile figures on the right, said her husband had been "beloved."

items.title: Trump says there will either be a deal with Iran or US will 'finish the job'
items.summary: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump said on Monday the United States would either reach a deal with Iran or "finish the job," renewing his threat of military action as Teheran projects defiance following the funeral of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. "We're either going to make a deal or we're going to finish the job. Secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council Mohammad Baqer Zolqadr called Trump's threat "delusional."

items.title: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs as it revamps Xbox
items.summary: WASHINGTON: Microsoft said Monday it was eliminating about 4,800 jobs – roughly two percent of its global workforce – in a cost-cutting move that will deliver a sweeping restructuring of its struggling Xbox gaming division. The cuts include the deepest overhaul in Xbox's history, with approximately 3,200 gaming jobs to be shed over the coming fiscal year, including positions at four studios that are being spun off or sold. Coleman said the layoffs fell mostly within Microsoft's commercial business and Xbox.

items.title: Johor polls: Pakatan’s Pasir Raja candidate courts youth and Felda settlers using hybrid approach
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, July 6 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Pasir Raja state constituency, Mohd Fakharuddin Moslim is using a so-called hybrid strategy to win over voters with just four days left before polling day for the Johor state election. He said the hybrid approach combined aggressive face-to-face campaigning on the ground with dominance of narratives on social media platforms, a method that proved effective as his machinery successfully completed a 100 per cent tour across all localities in the state constituency. In terms of physical coverage, our machinery had successfully traced the entire Pasir Raja state constituency, including relatively remote areas such as Sungai Redan.

items.title: Dewan Rakyat passes Bill to strengthen MyCC powers to tackle cartels and monopolies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — The Dewan Rakyat today passed the Competition Commission (Amendment) Bill 2026, aimed at strengthening the Malaysia Competition Commission (MyCC) and ensuring comprehensive enforcement of the country’s competition law framework. In his winding-up speech, Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the bill, which contained 34 amendments, was important in strengthening a fair, transparent and dynamic market amid increasingly complex cartel activities. Through the latest amendments, he said MyCC’s powers to require the provision of information had been extended to include the implementation of market reviews.

items.title: Health minister aims for all housemen to get permanent posts by 2028
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 6 — The Health Ministry (MOH) targets to offer permanent posts to all housemen immediately upon completing their training by 2028, said Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad. “By 2028, MOH targets to offer permanent posts to all housemen immediately upon completing their training. This year alone, 4,500 contract medical officers will be absorbed into permanent roles, alongside 800 new positions approved annually,” he said.

items.title: Banting school stabbing: Student stable after hospital treatment
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 6 — A student injured in a stabbing incident at a secondary school in Banting, Kuala Langat, is in stable condition after receiving emergency treatment at Hospital Banting, the Selangor Health Department said today. In a statement, the department said the student arrived at the hospital’s Emergency Department at 10.13am and was immediately attended to by the medical team. “The victim was given emergency treatment without delay.

items.title: Storm alert for Selangor, KL, Johor, other states until 9PM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit eight states and Kuala Lumpur until 9 pm tonight. According to a warning issued by the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) this evening, the states affected are Perak (Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Kinta, Kampar, Batang Padang and Mualim) and Kelantan (Jeli and Kuala Krai). Similar conditions are also expected to hit Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Raub, Bentong and Rompin), Selangor (Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat), Negeri Sembilan (Seremban and Jelebu) and Johor (Segamat, Kluang, Mersing, Kulai, Kota Tinggi and Johor Bahru).

items.title: Three arrested for robbing migrant worker while posing as police in Manjung
items.summary: IPOH, July 6 — Police have busted a robbery syndicate that impersonated policemen with the arrest of three local men within hours of their alleged robbery of a Bangladeshi man in Manjung last Thursday. Perak Police Chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the 45-year-old victim lodged a police report at 8.06 pm, claiming he had been robbed by two local men who identified themselves as policemen in an incident at Pantai Remis at about 4.30 pm the same day. “The suspects had threatened to take the victim to the Pantai Remis Police Station for further inspection.

items.title: Sentul swoop uncovers World Cup betting hub doubling as unlicensed entertainment outlet, 33 foreigners detained
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — Police raided a premises in Taman Wahyu, Sentul, yesterday, believed to be used as a 2026 World Cup football betting call centre as well as an unlicensed entertainment outlet, detaining 34 individuals, including 33 foreigners. Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the raid was conducted at 3.30am by the Gambling, Vice and Secret Societies Division (D7) of the Sentul district police headquarters' Criminal Investigation Department, following surveillance and intelligence gathering. “Initial investigations found the premises were used to manage, organise, and promote online gambling involving 2026 World Cup football betting, besides operating as an unlicensed entertainment outlet.

items.title: Woman fined RM500 after admitting using criminal force against Perak Sultan during state-level National Day event
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — A woman who admitted using criminal force against the Sultan of Perak during last year’s state-level National Day celebrations was fined RM500 by the Magistrates’ Court in Ipoh today. According to Sinar Harian , Magistrate Norizan Riduan imposed the fine after Nurhaswani Afni Mohamad Zorki, 42, pleaded guilty to the charge. The court also ordered her to serve one month’s imprisonment if she fails to pay the fine.

items.title: PMO proposed every Jana Wibawa contractor, Tengku Zafrul tells ex-PM Muhyiddin’s trial
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — All of the Malaysian government’s projects under the Muhyiddin administration’s Jana Wibawa programme were awarded to contractors that had been proposed directly by the Prime Minister’s Office (PMO), former finance minister Datuk Seri Tengku Zafrul Tengku Abdul Aziz said today. Tengku Zafrul said this while testifying as the 11th prosecution witness in former prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin’s power abuse and money laundering trial in connection to the Jana Wibawa project. “Throughout the Jana Wibawa programme, all contractor companies that were awarded projects are companies that had been proposed by the Prime Minister’s Office only.

items.title: Sitiawan raids reveal 208 live bullets and weapons cache as police move on trafficking suspect
items.summary: IPOH, July 6 — Police seized 208 live rounds of ammunition and several objects resembling firearms, besides arresting a local man in two raids linked to drug trafficking activities in Sitiawan, near Manjung, last Wednesday. Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the operation conducted by the Perak Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (NCID) also resulted in the seizure of drugs believed to be Methylenedioxymethamphetamine (MDMA) and ketamine, with a total estimated value of RM6,547. He said said following the arrest, police raided a house in Jalan Kampung Cina, Sitiawan, where they found 12 transparent plastic packets containing substances believed to be ketamine, weighing 64.30 grammes.

items.title: High Court sets Oct 12 showdown as MCMC takes Papagomo to task over censorship talk
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — The High Court today fixed October 12 to hear an inter partes interim injunction application by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) against blogger Wan Muhammad Azri Wan Deris, better known as Papagomo, in a defamation suit. The court also extends the ad interim injunction,” the judge said. Earlier, MCMC counsel Nurshafiqa Balqish Jaffri informed the court that Wan Muhammad Azri had entered an appearance in the suit.

items.title: Victim’s care, school support are top priority after Banting stabbing, says education minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the government’s immediate priority is ensuring medical treatment for the student injured in a stabbing at a secondary school in Banting, while providing psychosocial support for the wider school community as police investigate the incident. In a Facebook post after visiting the school today, Fadhlina said the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) had taken security measures to bring the situation under control, including arresting the suspect. “The priority now is to ensure the victim receives medical treatment, while the Ministry of Education’s priority is to provide a psychosocial support system for the school community.

items.title: Sabah minister flags unlicensed centres, urges mums and dads to stick with approved childcare
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 6 — State Women, Health and People’s Wellbeing Minister Datuk Julita Majungki has urged parents to send their children to registered childcare centres to safeguard their safety and wellbeing, while highlighting the need to expand childcare capacity in Sabah. She said the installation of closed-circuit television (CCTV) systems is a mandatory requirement for registered childcare centres and an important measure to help ensure children's safety. Speaking at the closing ceremony of the Sabah State-Level Childcare Centre Day Celebration here on Sunday, Julita said Sabah has only about 230 registered childcare centres despite an estimated 445,000 children aged up to four by 2030 requiring around 110,000 childcare places, describing investment in early childhood development as vital for families, human capital and the State's future.

items.title: ‘Give agriculture centre stage’: DOSM chief urges stronger domestic output to secure Malaysia’s food future
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 6 — Malaysia recorded a score of 61.5 in the Malaysian Food Security Index 2024, an increase from 54.5 in 2023, indicating that the country’s food security level is on the right track. Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) chief statistician Datuk Seri Dr. Mohd Uzir Mahidin said that from the four main dimensions evaluated, accessibility recorded the highest score of 79.9, followed by stability (54.6), usability (50.4) and availability (50). He said that the high accessibility score indicates that Malaysia does not face problems in obtaining food supplies because the shortfall in domestic production can still be compensated via imports.

items.title: Umno deputy president dismisses Najib link claims, says Johor election about state government not pardons
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 6 — The Johor state election campaign must be conducted maturely without personal attacks to ensure national stability is preserved, said Umno deputy president Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan. “Let us not be overly personal. Our parties are fine, nothing personal.

items.title: DOSM: Govt to develop Malaysia’s first cost-of-living benchmark for families caring for disabled and elderly dependants
items.summary: According to Sinar Harian , the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) is developing a Basic Expenditure for Decent Living (PAKW) benchmark tailored to households with special care needs, the first of its kind in the country. Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Mohd Uzir Mahidin said the initiative would measure the actual cost of living borne by these households while serving as an important reference for the government in designing financial assistance, subsidies, caregiving support and social protection programmes. “It also includes patients requiring home-based care, whether bedridden or not, as well as older persons who require specialised care,” Mohd Uzir said.

items.title: Witness: Schoolgirl stabbed after buying bread at canteen during Banting school recess
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 6 — A routine morning recess at a secondary school in Banting was abruptly interrupted when a Form Three student was allegedly stabbed moments after buying bread at the school canteen. A male student who was outside the school after the incident told the national daily that the suspect had approached the canteen from the direction of the school’s surau shortly before the attack. “The incident happened during recess for Form Three students.

items.title: Malaysia eyes overland durian exports to China through Thailand, says Mat Sabu
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 6 — Malaysia is in talks with the Thai government and the General Administration of Customs of China (GACC) to establish a land route for exporting local durians to China, a move expected to reduce logistics costs and expand market access in the country. Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said the initiative is part of the government’s efforts to support durian growers, particularly amid a current glut following the durian season, which has occurred simultaneously in several states. On food security, Mohamad said Malaysia still needs to raise its self-sufficiency levels for several key commodities, including rice, meat, and maize for animal feed.

items.title: Banting schoolgirl stabbed up to 16 times, suffered punctured lung, father says
items.summary: KUALA KUMPUR, July 6 — A schoolgirl injured in a stabbing at a secondary school in Banting was stabbed up to 16 times and suffered a punctured lung, her father said today, as police continued investigating the attack. Online , the victim’s father, Mohd Firuz Ibrahim, 43, said he received a call from one of his daughter’s teachers at about 9.30am before rushing to Hospital Banting, where she is being treated. Mohd Firuz said most of the wounds were to his daughter’s back.

items.title: Customs officer pleads not guilty to RM15,000 bribery charge in Shah Alam court
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 6 — An assistant director with the Royal Malaysian Customs Department pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to receiving RM15,000 in bribes last month. MACC Deputy Public Prosecutor Muaz Ahmad Khairuddin proposed bail of RM10,000, subject to additional conditions that the accused surrender his passport to the court, report to a nearby MACC office and refrain from contacting prosecution witnesses. Lawyer Azrul Zulkifli Stork, representing Fareez Akmal, asked the court to consider bail of RM5,000, arguing that his client supports a family and elderly parents in poor health.

items.title: MB: Perak to review Sejahtera 2030 blueprint as state updates development agenda
items.summary: IPOH, July 6 — The Perak government will carry out a mid-term review of its Perak Sejahtera 2030 blueprint to ensure the state's long-term development agenda remains relevant and on track to achieve its 2030 goals. Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said the review would include refining monitoring indicators, strengthening the blueprint's agenda and flagship initiatives, as well as updating the Perak Sejahtera 2030 Dashboard. Saarani said the review was intended to ensure the state's development blueprint remained dynamic and responsive to changing economic conditions and development needs.
